adminSecurityNotice: The static admin panel is a convenience review dashboard, not real authentication. It must not contain patient data, secrets, or private health information.

items.title: The New-Year Smile Reset: What to Do Before You Book Whitening, Aligners, or Cosmetic Dental Work
sourceBasis: Tufts Now: young adults skipping dental care
American Dental Association: harmful social media dental trends
MouthHealthy: dental trends and DIY dentistry warnings
ADA whitening topic page
CDC oral health equity and prevention resources
CareQuest 2025 oral health access findings

sources.title: One in Three Young Adults Skip the Dentist—and That's a Problem
sources.supports: Young adult dental avoidance, cost, and access context.

sources.title: Social media dental trend warnings
sources.supports: General patient education and caution around DIY dental trends.

sources.title: Oral Health Basics and Prevention
sources.supports: General preventive oral health context.

items.quickAnswer: The New-Year Smile Reset: What to Do Before You Book Whitening, Aligners, or Cosmetic Dental Work is not about chasing a perfect smile or letting the internet talk you into a treatment you do not understand. It is about slowing the decision down long enough to protect your health, your money, your enamel, and your confidence. A confident smile starts with information. Before whitening, bonding, veneers, aligners, or any other cosmetic conversation, the first question is not, “What looks good online?” The first question is, “What is actually happening with my teeth and gums right.
items.usefulTakeaway: Start with a real exam, ask clear questions, and make dental decisions from health, timing, and confidence instead of internet pressure.

items.quickAnswer: A long-form guide for informed, confidence-centered dental decisions. Treatment decisions should be discussed with a licensed dental professional after an exam. This is general education only, not diagnosis or a treatment recommendation. It is designed to help patients slow down decisions, understand common options, prepare better appointment questions, and remember that treatment recommendations require an exam with a licensed dental professional.
items.usefulTakeaway: Start with a real exam, ask clear questions, and make dental decisions from health, timing, and confidence instead of internet pressure.

queryIntelligence.policy: Uses public question patterns and manual/social observations. Raw social posts, usernames, patient data, and private health information are not stored.
queryIntelligence.nextAutomationStep: Month-13 auto-drafting can create ready_for_approval drafts only after an approved LLM provider key is configured.
